even.e: The teacher gave each student even pieces of the cake.

every.d: Each one of a group of things
every.e: I eat every meal at home.

each.e: Each child in the class has a new book.

end.d: the finish or conclusion of something
end.e: I'll reach the end of the book soon.

ever.e: I will love my family ever and always.

everything.d: All the things, the whole world
everything.e: I want to learn about everything in the world.

enough.d: As much as is needed, not too little or too much
enough.e: I have enough toys to play with.

early.d: before the usual or expected time
early.e: I woke up early to eat breakfast.

everyone.e: Everyone in the class raised their hand.

else.e: I can't find my sweater, so I'll wear something else.

example.d: something that shows what other things are like
example.e: The teacher gave us an example of how to solve the math problem.

either.d: One or the other, but not both
either.e: I can't decide, so I'll take either one.

education.d: The process of learning new things at school
education.e: My education helps me learn and grow.

experience.d: Something that happens to you or that you do
experience.e: I had a fun experience at the zoo.

easy.d: Not hard to do or understand
easy.e: This math worksheet is easy for me to complete.

english.d: The language spoken in many countries
english.e: My family speaks English at home.

especially.d: Meaning 'more than usual' or 'above all else'
especially.e: I like pizza especially when it has lots of cheese on top.

eyes.d: The things on your face that you use to see
eyes.e: My eyes are green like the grass.

energy.d: The power that makes things move or work
energy.e: Eating a healthy snack gives me more energy to play.

evidence.d: Proof that something is true or really happened
evidence.e: The muddy footprints were evidence that someone had been in the house.

economic.d: Related to money, jobs, and the way a country makes and uses money
economic.e: The economic growth of the town has been good.

european.e: Many European countries have old castles.

east.d: The direction where the sun rises
east.e: The sun sets in the west and rises in the east.

event.e: My birthday party is a fun event I look forward to.

eat.d: To put food in your mouth and swallow it
eat.e: I eat an apple for a healthy snack.

england.d: A country that is part of the United Kingdom.
england.e: The family went on vacation to England last summer.

europe.d: A continent that includes many countries.
europe.e: Europe has many beautiful cities to visit.

events.d: Things that happen, especially important or fun ones
events.e: The school is having a lot of exciting events this month.

exactly.e: The teacher wanted us to answer the question exactly right.

earth.e: The earth has oceans, mountains, and forests.

effect.d: The result or change caused by something.
effect.e: The new medicine had a good effect on my illness.

entire.d: All of something, without anything missing
entire.e: I ate the entire cookie in one bite.

election.d: When people vote to choose a leader
election.e: There is an important election happening soon

enjoy.d: To have fun with something and like it a lot
enjoy.e: I really enjoy playing at the park with my friends.

except.e: Everyone in my family went to the zoo, except my little brother.

eight.d: The number that comes after seven
eight.e: I have eight fingers on my hands.

established.d: When something has been around for a long time
established.e: The school has been established for over 50 years.

earlier.e: I went to the park earlier today.

expected.d: When something is likely to happen
expected.e: I expected the rain to come, so I brought my umbrella.

eye.d: The part of your body that you use to see
eye.e: I can see the bird with my eye.

etc.e: The store had apples, oranges, bananas, etc.

expect.e: I expect it will rain today.

effects.d: The results of something that happened
effects.e: The new medicine had some good effects on my cold.

economy.d: The way a country makes and spends money
economy.e: Our economy is doing well this year.

effort.d: the hard work you put into something
effort.e: I put a lot of effort into cleaning my room.

explain.d: to tell someone about something clearly
explain.e: Can you explain how to do the math problem?

easily.d: without a lot of trouble or difficulty
easily.e: I can easily ride my bike to the park.

environment.d: the natural world around us, like plants and animals

everybody.d: all the people in a group
everybody.e: Everybody in my family likes to go to the park.

ended.d: When something is finished or has come to a close.
ended.e: The school day ended at 3 o'clock.

extra.d: Something that is more than what is needed or usual.
extra.e: I had an extra cookie after dinner.

evening.d: The time of day after the sun goes down
evening.e: We eat dinner together as a family in the evening.

eventually.d: Happening after a long time or a series of events
eventually.e: I will eventually finish my homework assignment.

exchange.d: To give one thing and get something else in return
exchange.e: I can exchange my old books for new ones.

enter.e: I enter the classroom when the bell rings.

effective.d: Working well to produce a desired result
effective.e: The new medicine was effective at making me feel better.

executive.d: A person who is in charge of a company or organization
executive.e: The executive of the company made an important decision.

efforts.d: The hard work and energy you use to do something.
efforts.e: The soccer team made great efforts to win the game.

engine.d: The part of a car, truck, or other vehicle that makes it move.
engine.e: The car's engine stopped working, so we had to call for help.

episode.d: A single part of a TV show or story.
episode.e: I watched the latest episode of my favorite cartoon last night.

et.d: A shortened way to say 'and' or 'also'.
et.e: I love chocolate chip cookies et ice cream.

eating.e: I am eating an apple for a snack.

el.e: El perro es mi mascota favorita.

equipment.d: The things you need to do something, like tools or devices.
equipment.e: My dad bought new equipment for our camping trip.

eastern.d: Coming from or belonging to the east
eastern.e: The sun rose in the eastern sky.

ed.d: A way to make a word past tense
ed.e: The dog walked and played all day.

ex.d: A person who was your boyfriend or girlfriend
ex.e: Her ex-boyfriend came to the party.

exist.d: To be real and to be present
exist.e: Dinosaurs used to exist a long time ago.

elements.d: The basic parts that make up something.
elements.e: The main elements of a cake are flour, eggs, and sugar.

eu.d: Short for 'European Union', a group of countries in Europe.
eu.e: The EU has many rules that the countries follow.

emergency.d: A serious situation that needs help right away.
emergency.e: If there is an emergency, we need to call 911 for help.

ends.d: The last part or point of something.
ends.e: The game ends when the timer reaches zero.

estate.d: A large piece of land with a big house on it.
estate.e: My grandparents live on a big estate with lots of trees and flowers.

edge.d: The outer part or boundary of something.
edge.e: I was careful not to fall off the edge of the cliff.

employees.d: People who work for a company or organization.
employees.e: The store has many friendly employees to help customers.

en.d: A short way to say 'in' in some languages.
en.e: The book is written en español.

enemy.d: A person or group that you are fighting against.
enemy.e: The team's enemy tried to score more points.

entered.d: To go into or come into a place.
entered.e: I entered the room and sat down.

excellent.d: Very good, the best, or of the highest quality.
excellent.e: The teacher said my drawing was excellent!

equal.e: The two cakes are equal in size.

exercise.d: Physical activity to keep your body healthy
exercise.e: I exercise by running every day.

existing.e: My family is existing happily in our new home.

easier.e: The new math homework was easier than the last assignment.

edition.d: A version or copy of something
edition.e: The library has the latest edition of the book.

em.d: A short way to write the word 'them'
em.e: I'm going to the park with em.

ensure.d: To make sure something happens or is done.
ensure.e: I will ensure that I finish my homework tonight.

email.d: A way to send messages to people using a computer or phone.
email.e: The teacher sent an email to all the students.

express.d: To show or communicate your thoughts and feelings.
express.e: The artist expressed their creativity through their painting.

external.d: Something that is outside or not part of the inside.
external.e: The external temperature was very cold today.

environmental.d: relating to the natural world and how we take care of it
environmental.e: Recycling is good for the environmental.

everywhere.d: in all places or in many different places
everywhere.e: The children looked for their lost toy everywhere.

engineering.d: The science of designing and building things like bridges and machines
engineering.e: The engineers are working on a new design for the school's playground.

escape.d: To get away from a dangerous or difficult situation
escape.e: The family had to escape the fire by climbing out the window.

examples.d: Things that show what something is like
examples.e: The teacher gave us some examples to help us understand the math problem.

extended.e: The school gave the students an extended break for the holidays.

excited.d: Feeling happy and eager about something.
excited.e: The child was excited to open their birthday present.

expensive.d: Something that costs a lot of money.
expensive.e: The new toy was very expensive to buy.

extent.d: How big or how much of something there is.
extent.e: The extent of the damage was greater than expected.

emotional.e: The girl was emotional when she won the game.

entry.d: A piece of information written in a book or list
entry.e: The teacher wrote the homework entry in the class journal.

estimated.d: To guess the value or amount of something
estimated.e: The teacher estimated there were 25 students in the class.

experienced.d: Having done or seen something many times before
experienced.e: The firefighter had a lot of experience putting out fires.

explained.d: To describe something so it is easy to understand
explained.e: The teacher explained the math problem to the class.

elected.d: chosen by voting to have a job or position
elected.e: The class president was elected by the students.

employment.d: the act of having a job
employment.e: My dad has been in employment at the same company for 10 years.

ending.e: The movie had a happy ending.

era.d: a long period of time in history
era.e: The dinosaurs lived in the Jurassic era.

error.d: a mistake or something done wrong
error.e: I made an error on my math homework.

expressed.d: to show or say what you are thinking or feeling
expressed.e: The artist expressed her feelings through her painting.

educational.d: Something that helps you learn new things.
educational.e: The museum had many educational exhibits for the students to enjoy.

essential.d: Something that is very important and necessary.
essential.e: Drinking water is essential for staying healthy.

existence.d: When something is real and alive.
existence.e: The existence of the new baby brought great joy to the family.

expression.d: A look or gesture that shows how you are feeling.
expression.e: The child's happy expression showed how much they enjoyed the party.

electric.e: The electric car is fast and quiet.

extreme.d: Something that is the most or the least of its kind
extreme.e: Climbing the tallest mountain is an extreme challenge.

empty.e: The box was completely empty when I opened it.

experiences.d: Things that happen to a person that they learn from
experiences.e: Going to the zoo was a fun experience for my family.

egypt.d: A country in Africa with ancient history

expansion.d: When something gets bigger or grows.
expansion.e: The company plans an expansion to a new location.

editor.d: A person who is responsible for organizing and preparing written material.
editor.e: The magazine editor carefully reviewed all the articles before publishing.

empire.d: A group of countries or territories that are all controlled by one ruler or government.
empire.e: The Roman Empire was a very powerful empire that lasted for many centuries.

engaged.d: When someone is busy doing an activity or is focused on something.
engaged.e: The students were engaged in their work and didn't want to stop.

entitled.d: Having the right or privilege to do or have something.
entitled.e: As a student, I'm entitled to use the school library and computers.

expert.d: A person who has a lot of knowledge and skill in a particular area.
expert.e: The scientist is an expert on the local wildlife in our neighborhood.

electronic.d: Something that uses electricity and technology.
electronic.e: The computer is an electronic device.

enjoyed.d: When you have fun and like doing something.
enjoyed.e: The kids enjoyed playing at the park.

equivalent.d: When two things are the same or equal in value.
equivalent.e: Ten dimes are equivalent to one dollar.

exists.d: When something is real and present.
exists.e: The sun exists in our solar system.

establish.d: To create or set up something new
establish.e: We need to establish a plan for our class trip.

elsewhere.e: The cat ran elsewhere in the house.

entertainment.d: Activities or shows that people enjoy watching or doing.
entertainment.e: The circus was great entertainment for the family.

excuse.d: A reason for not doing something.
excuse.e: I had an excuse for not finishing my homework on time.

earned.d: to get money or a reward for work or effort
earned.e: The boy earned a new toy by doing his chores.

eggs.d: The round things that chickens lay that you can eat
eggs.e: Mom made scrambled eggs for breakfast.

element.e: Water is an important element for keeping plants healthy.

exciting.d: Something that is fun and interesting
exciting.e: Going on a field trip to the zoo was an exciting day for the class.

exposed.d: When something is out in the open or can be seen
exposed.e: The cat's fur was exposed to the rain when it went outside.

extensive.d: Something that is very large or covers a wide area
extensive.e: The family went on an extensive hike through the mountains.

edward.d: A person's name, like the name of a boy or a girl

effectively.d: In a way that works well or is successful
effectively.e: The teacher explained the math problem effectively.

establishment.d: A place where people work or do business
establishment.e: The restaurant is a popular establishment in our town.

evolution.d: The way living things change and grow over time
evolution.e: The evolution of the butterfly from a caterpillar is amazing.

exact.d: Completely correct and without any mistakes
exact.e: I need to give the exact change for my purchase.

exclusive.d: Something that only certain people can have or do
exclusive.e: The hotel has an exclusive swimming pool for guests only.

experts.d: People who have a lot of knowledge about a subject
experts.e: The scientists are the experts on this new discovery.

extension.d: Something that makes something else bigger or longer
extension.e: The teacher gave me an extension to finish my homework.

employed.d: When you have a job and are working.
employed.e: She was employed as a teacher at the school.

explanation.d: a way of making something clear or easy to understand
explanation.e: The teacher's explanation of the math problem helped the students.

egg.d: The oval-shaped object that birds lay and hatch into baby birds
egg.e: The chicken laid a fresh egg in its nest.
